{{Infobox character
| name = Kelarac
| appears_in = The Elder Empire: Sea, The Elder Empire: Shadow
| titles = Collector of Souls, Great Elder, Father of Merchants, Lord of Coin, Gambler’s Delight, Miser’s Bane”,
| abilities = Reader of Memory, grant wishes, uses dreams
| introduced = ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow
}}

''See also: [[Kelarac Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Kelarac, known as the [[Soul Collector]], is a highly respected [[Great Elder]] distinguished by his lack of a specific territory. Unlike his siblings, he wanders through various domains, gathering power from humans by granting wishes in exchange for valuable items such as artifacts, relics, blood, sanity, or personal loyalty. While he is viewed by some groups, like the [[Sleepless]], as a potential bridge for interaction between humans and non-humans due to his willingness to assist, he is also feared as a shrewd negotiator whose bargains often lead to the ruin of the other party. He maintains that he is bound by his nature to honor his agreements. Historically, Kelarac's true appearance is remembered only by [[the Emperor]]. He has been described as appearing like a fashionable [[Heartlander]] with a trimmed beard, many rings, and jewels, though he is also noted for having a [[steel blindfold]] bolted over his eyes. In other forms or legends, he is depicted as a massive ocean creature with the appearance of a shark and stony skin. He is currently said to be chained within a drowned city beneath the [[Aion]], though reports of his presence persist. Individuals seeking his attention may attempt to summon him through focused [[Intent]] and a sacrifice of something valuable tossed into the [[Aion]], though such efforts do not guarantee a response.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Kelarac is a [[Great Elder]] and [[Reader of Memory]] known as the Collector of Souls who resides at the bottom of the sea. As the origin of the [[Lyathatan]], this being is chained to a drowned city beneath the [[Aion]] and possesses a deep, fathomless mind. Kelarac is characterized by dark skin, ringed fingers, jeweled hands, and a gold-capped smile, though his head is shrouded in [[steel]] with a metal band bolted over his eyes to act as a blindfold. Though he is the only [[Great Elder]] without a specific domain, he gathers power from humans by granting wishes in exchange for valuable items. A shrewd bargainer whose deals often doom the other party, Kelarac entered into a bargain with [[Calder]], an event that allowed [[the Emperor]] to sense his presence upon the man.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is known as the Collector of Souls.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c2">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is said to take souls.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c6">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is a being residing at the bottom of the sea.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c7">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is the origin of the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c11">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] made a bargain with [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c22">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is a [[Great Elder]] whose name means ‘Collector of Souls’.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has a reputation as a shrewd bargainer whose deals often doom the other party.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] was the only known [[Great Elder]] without a specific domain.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] gathers power from humans by granting wishes in exchange for items of value.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is chained to a drowned city beneath the [[Aion]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has been called the Father of Merchants, the [[Lord]] of Coin, the Gambler’s Delight, Miser’s Bane, the Keeper of the Vaults, the Hoard-gatherer, and the [[Seeker]] of Treasures.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] wears a silver metal band over his eyes that acts as a [[steel blindfold]] bolted into his sockets.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has dark skin and the poise of a [[Heartlander]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is a [[Great Elder]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] is a [[Reader of Memory]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has ringed fingers.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has a gold-capped smile.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has a [[steel]]-shrouded head.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] wears a [[steel blindfold]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has jeweled hands.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] has a deep and fathomless mind.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[The Emperor]] senses [[Kelarac]] upon [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Kelarac]] could be said to grant wishes.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kelarac]] is rumored to wait at the bottom of the [[Aion]] alongside [[Nakothi]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c16">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kelarac]] keeps the souls of those who fall afoul of his deals.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c17">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]] would send [[The Testament]] down to [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c27">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|Once a fashionable [[Heartlander]] characterized by gold teeth and a thin beard, Kelarac is a Great One known as the [[Soul Collector]]. Although he is currently sealed at the bottom of the ocean, he remains free to act and maintains a service tied to the [[Lyathatan]]. Through the use of dreams, he provided [[Calder]] with an [[Awakened blade]] for combat against [[Elderspawn]] and has been associated with the sending of ships. This entity claims to possess knowledge regarding human [[Intent]], the future, and the secrets of worlds and time. Even while submerged, his presence is felt through his will and a visage so distorted that his cheeks appear like a mask stapled to a face.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] has the approval of [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is still free to act.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is a figure whose service is tied to the [[Lyathatan]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is a place or entity mentioned in relation to going down.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] gave [[Calder]] an [[Awakened blade]] to use against [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] uses dreams.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is a person mentioned in relation to sending ships.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c9">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is a Great One and the [[Soul Collector]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c10">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] claims to know the secrets of time, worlds, the future, and human [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] is known as the [[Soul Collector]] and is sealed at the bottom of the ocean.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]]'s face was so distorted that his cheeks looked like a mask stapled onto someone else's head.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Lucan]] compares the experience of using the [[Optasia]] to staring into the eyes of [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jerri]] perceives [[Kelarac]]'s will.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] was singlehandedly sent a rebel fleet down to [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c18">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Kelarac]] was a fashionable [[Heartlander]] with a thin beard and gold teeth.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c22">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|Kelarac is a figure associated with the ancient era of the [[Great Elders]]. This eyeless being is linked to [[the Emperor]]'s capacity to [[Read]] and is a creature that could potentially rise from the sea. Looking toward this entity, [[Estyr]] intends to send [[Calder Marten]] down to Kelarac.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kelarac]] is mentioned in relation to [[the Emperor]]'s ability to [[Read]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c12">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kelarac]] does not have eyes.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c15">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kelarac]] is a figure associated with the era of the [[Great Elders]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c16">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kelarac]] is a being that could potentially rise from the sea.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c19">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Estyr]] intends to send [[Calder Marten]] down to [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c22">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Kelarac is an immense, living ocean creature capable of blotting out the sun and is associated with an [[Elder]]. This entity possesses infinite knowledge and maintains a connection to [[Calder]], having left a warm, six-fingered handprint burned into the man's forearm. Appearing with a shark's head, silvery teeth, and a tower-sized dorsal fin, Kelarac's bronze, craggy body resembles an intricate statue or an ancient stone wall. While wearing a fire-reflecting blindfold, the being projects a powerful force of will and utilizes a mark to sense [[Intent]]. Kelarac is known to keep his bargains and lace his lies with truth, though he has warned [[Calder]] regarding the limitations of the [[Awakened blade]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] left a six-fingered handprint burned into [[Calder]]'s forearm.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c2">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] has [[Kelarac]]’s warm handprint on his arm.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] left a handprint on [[Calder]]'s skin.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] has a connection to [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is said to collect anything thrown into the [[Aion Sea]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c10">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] has felt the force of will projected by [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c11">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Lyathatan]] is a creature of [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] possesses infinite knowledge.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is someone [[Cheska]] mentions in a threat.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c16">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] addresses [[Calder]] as [[Reader of Memory]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] wears a blindfold with rivets that reflect fire.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]]'s shadow contains a thousand unseen shapes and blinking eyes.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] once threatened [[Jerri]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is known to keep his bargains.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] laced his lies with truth.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has a gold-ringed hand.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]]'s body is bronze and craggy like an intricate statue.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has a shark's head with silvery, triangular teeth.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has a dorsal fin the size of a tower.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has a mark that can be used to sense [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] warned [[Calder]] about the limitations of the [[Awakened blade]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] stated that there is a law beyond this world that binds even their kind.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is alive.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is described as a fool rather than a puppet.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is an ocean creature large enough to blot out the sun.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]]'s skin resembles an ancient stone wall.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has ghostly chains.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] is a location or entity associated with an [[Elder]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] needs [[Calder]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]]'s power involves an [[Intent]] of greed and possession.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has a glossy brown braid.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]] has thin caramel-skinned hands.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Kelarac]]'s war is one name for the battle against [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c26">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Kelarac is a shark-like creature with stony skin and a rusted [[steel blindfold]] bolted to his face who has previously been blinded and nailed to the floor of the [[Aion Sea]]. A person who enjoys buying people and marking his favorite slaves, he holds influence over [[the Emperor]] and maintains control of the [[Empire]]. He is linked to [[Calder Marten]], whom he allegedly enthralled and used as a pawn, and may have even restored the man to [[life]]. As Kelarac approached, he was viewed as a threat requiring [[Soulbound]] to fight, though he remained open to negotiation and the offering of deals. Ultimately, after being devoured from the inside by energy, Kelarac died, leaving his power to live only in [[Shera]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] likes to buy people.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c6">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] was blinded and nailed to the floor of the [[Aion Sea]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] placed a mark on his favorite slaves.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is mentioned by [[Estyr]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is a person [[Calder Marten]] was accused of consorting with.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] holds the reins of the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] has influence over [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is a person whom [[Calder Marten]] is allegedly enthralled by.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Calder Marten]] is a pawn of [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Calder]] was marked by [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c20">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] gave [[Jyrine]] a gift that had a happy and muted song.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is a location that can be returned to.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]]'s actions involve fuel and risk.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is a threat that the city needs to fight.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Soulbound]] are needed to fight [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Jorin]] believes what is coming is worse than [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is open to negotiation.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Urg’naut]] stated he is not [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c23">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Urg'naut mentioned that [[Kelarac]] is coming.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is on his way and may be on the same level as the [[Creeping Shadow]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] may have restored [[Calder Marten]] to [[life]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is described as a fool by [[Loreli]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is capable of offering deals.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The first challenge in the war against [[Kelarac]] was fitting everyone aboard [[Navigator ships]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Bareius]] was preparing for a battle with [[Kelarac]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is a shark-like creature with stony skin and a [[steel blindfold]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] has a rusted [[steel blindfold]] bolted to his face.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] can take a stone shark form.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] was devoured from the inside by energy turning on its origin.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is a source of energy for [[Shera]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] claims he can be put into an object and thrown into the ocean to become no threat for centuries.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c26">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]]'s power lives only in [[Shera]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] has an arsenal of weapons.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Kelarac]] is dead.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c27">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 27</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|“Kelarac is still free to act,” Jerri said.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“I’m pleased to see you again, Reader of Memory,” Kelarac said.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“Kelarac keeps his bargains,” Jerri responded.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“Kelarac is alive and we think he’s coming here. The sky is…wide open.”<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|It cost Kelarac to do this,” Jorin went on. “In fuel and in risk.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Readers]]
